title: Note du dépositaire - entrée en vigueur de l'accord pour certains signataires

pages.text:
Ambassade de France

Aux Etets - Unis

AUG 6 1952

classeur

The Secretary of State presents his compliments to

Their Excellencies and Messieurs the Chiefs of Mission of the Governments signatory to the Treaty of Peace with

Japan, signed at San Francisco on September 8, 1951, and has the honor to refer to the Department's circular note of June 10, 1952 regarding the opening for signature on

June 12, 1952 of the Agreement for the Settlement of

Disputes Arising under Article 15(a) of the Treaty of

Peace with Japan.

In accordance with the provisions of Article VITT of the above-mentioned Agreement, the Agreement (1) has been signed on behalf of the following Governments on the dates indicated and (2) has entered into force between the Government of Japan and certain of the signatory Governments on the dates indicated, as follows:

Government

Article IX of the Agreement provides that the Government of the United States of America shall furnish each signatory government with a certified copy thereof. In view of the fact that only twelve Governments have signed the Agreement, the preparation of facsimile certifled coples has been postponed in order that as many signatures as possible may appear on the certified copies. However, the

Governments signatory to the Agreement are requesting certified copies and it has been decided that the preparation of certified copies w111 begin on August 18, 1952.

Tt 1s

-3-

It is the hope of this Government, as depositary of the

Agreement, that other Governments will sign the Agreement before that date since it is contemplated that two certified copies of the Agreement will be sent to each of the Governments signatory to the Treaty of Peace with

Japan and that additional facsimile certified copies of .

the Agreement will not be prepared in the immediate future.

As stated in the Department's circular note of

June 10, 1952, this Agreement is open for signature in

State Annex no. 4, room 1078, and appointments may be made for signing it by telephoning Republic 5600, extension 2307•

The Secretary of State would be grateful if each

Chief of Mission would forward this information to his

Government.

Department of State,

Washington, July 31, 1952.

C10
